26.reason: [Incorrect usage of memset: arguments are in wrong order; should be memset(cnt, 0, sizeof(cnt)) or similar. Also, initializing to 1 byte-wise is not same as setting each int to 1.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Incorrect usage of memset: arguments are in wrong order; should be memset(cnt, 0, sizeof(cnt)) or similar. Also, initializing to 1 byte-wise is not same as setting each int to 1.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Arguments to memset are in wrong order; should be memset(cnt, 0, sizeof(cnt)) or similar. Current call sets only first byte of cnt to 1 and uses sizeof(int) as value, which is incorrect.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [The arguments to memset are in the wrong order; it should be memset(cnt, 0, sizeof(cnt)) or similar. Also, initializing with 1 sets each byte to 1, not each int to 1.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [Arguments to memset are in wrong order; should be memset(cnt, 0, sizeof(cnt)) or similar. Current call sets only first sizeof(int) bytes to 1, and doesn't initialize all elements properly.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [Arguments to memset are in wrong order; should be memset(cnt, 0, sizeof(cnt)) or similar. Current call sets only first sizeof(int) bytes to 1, and doesn't initialize all elements properly.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [Missing & operator before each variable in scanf; should be &l[i].p1.x etc. This causes undefined behavior as scanf expects pointers.]
